signals.idea:
Implement undo/redo/history system for tools/video-editor

## Context
The video editor uses React Context + custom hooks. All mutations flow through applyTimelineEdit() in useTimelineSave.ts. There is no existing undo/redo.

## Requirements

### Two-tier history system:

**Tier 1: Undo Stack (fine-grained, in-memory)**
- Push snapshot of { rows, meta, clipOrder, tracks, output } before every applyTimelineEdit() call
- Cap at ~50-100 entries
- Cmd+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z
- Drag operations and rapid edits should collapse into single undo entries (batch by user gesture using transaction ID or debounce window)

**Tier 2: Checkpoints (coarse-grained, persisted to Supabase)**
- Named snapshots of full TimelineConfig stored in Supabase DB
- Keep checkpoints for past 24 hours
- Browsable - jump to any checkpoint
- Activity-aware checkpoint triggers:
  1. Session boundary: When edit arrives after >5min idle gap, checkpoint pre-edit state
  2. Edit-distance cap: After ~30 edits since last checkpoint, auto-checkpoint
  3. Semantic triggers: Before destructive ops (delete track, bulk delete, clear timeline) + manual Save checkpoint
- Checkpoint metadata: id, timestamp, trigger type, auto-generated label, editsSinceLastCheckpoint, config

### UI
- Undo/redo buttons + history dropdown placed next to the Saving... indicator in the editor chrome
- History panel shows checkpoint list with labels and trigger types

### Persistence
- Checkpoints stored in Supabase (JSONB column or separate table) for past 24 hours
- Undo stack is in-memory only (session-level)

### Implementation approach
- New useTimelineHistory hook that wraps/intercepts applyTimelineEdit
- Exposes: undo(), redo(), jumpToCheckpoint(id), createCheckpoint(label)
- Manages idle timer via ref tracking lastEditTimestamp
- Checkpoints cap at ~20-30, undo stack at ~50-100

unresolved_flags.concern: Undo snapshots: commitData interception is too late to capture the true pre-edit state for drop flows that mutate editor state in place before calling `applyTimelineEdit`.
Checkpoints: semantic checkpoint plumbing does not actually reach the destructive track-management paths in this repo.
Undo batching: a single multi-clip track move from the keyboard would still produce one undo/save event per clip.
Testing: the plan only schedules manual validation even though this feature rewires the editor's core save path and the repository already uses automated tests for timeline hooks/context composition.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 7.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 4.

items: Cmd+Z undoes the most recent edit and restores the full previous timeline state (clips, tracks, positions, metadata)
Cmd+Shift+Z redoes after an undo, and redo stack is cleared when a new edit is made after undo
Dragging a clip collapses into a single undo entry (undo restores to pre-drag state, not intermediate positions)
Rapid slider/property edits within 300ms collapse into a single undo entry
Undo/redo buttons appear next to save badge, are disabled when stack is empty, and visually indicate availability
History dropdown shows checkpoint list with labels, trigger types, and timestamps
Checkpoints auto-create on session boundary (>5min idle gap), edit-distance cap (~30 edits), and before destructive operations
Manual 'Save checkpoint' creates a named checkpoint visible in the history panel
Jumping to a checkpoint fully restores the timeline to that saved state
Checkpoints persist across page reloads (stored in Supabase), limited to past 24 hours
Undo triggers a normal save to Supabase (the restored state is persisted)
No performance regression: undo stack snapshots stay under 2MB total, checkpoint saves are async

unresolved_flags.concern: Undo snapshots: commitData interception is too late to capture the true pre-edit state for drop flows that mutate editor state in place before calling `applyTimelineEdit`.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `handleAssetDrop` mutates `current.tracks` and `current.rows` before the save hook ever runs, then commits via `applyTimelineEdit` ([/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts#L156](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts#L156) to [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts#L165](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts#L165), [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts#L237](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts#L237) to [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts#L239](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts#L239)). External drops do the same for text drops and file drops ([/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts#L167](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts#L167) to [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts#L174](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts#L174), [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts#L194](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts#L194) to [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts#L202](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts#L202), [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts#L229](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts#L229)). A snapshot taken in `commitData` would already include the newly pushed track/row, so undo would not faithfully restore the pre-drop state.

unresolved_flags.concern: Undo batching: a single multi-clip track move from the keyboard would still produce one undo/save event per clip.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Arrow up/down already drive bulk movement through `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` ([/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts#L49](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts#L49) to [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts#L60](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts#L60)). That helper loops planned moves and calls `moveClipToRow` once per clip ([/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts#L296](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts#L296) to [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts#L298](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts#L298)), and each `moveClipToRow` commits through `applyTimelineEdit` ([/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts#L131](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts#L131) to [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts#L136](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts#L136)). The plan only batches drags and 300ms rapid edits, so one keypress on a 5-clip selection would still create 5 undo entries and 5 edit-distance increments.

unresolved_flags.concern: Testing: the plan only schedules manual validation even though this feature rewires the editor's core save path and the repository already uses automated tests for timeline hooks/context composition.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Phase 4 is manual-only, but the repo already has Vitest coverage patterns for timeline behavior in [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts#L1](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts#L1) to [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts#L122](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts#L122) and provider/context wiring in [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx#L1](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx#L1) to [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx#L58](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx#L58). With `useTimelineSave` as the persistence/conflict chokepoint ([/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts#L176](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts#L176) to [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts#L218](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts#L218)), omitting automated coverage leaves redo invalidation, semantic checkpointing, and conflict/save interactions uncontained.
